title: armeria maritima subsp. humilis
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. humilis, also known as dwarf sea thrift, is a low-growing, perennial plant species that belongs to the primrose family and is native to the coastal regions of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Armeria maritima subsp. humilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory problems, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in the form of essential oils, which can be applied topically to alleviate skin conditions and promote wound healing.
